clusters.title: 2 U.S. service members missing amid training exercise in Morocco
clusters.neutral_headline: Two US service members reported missing in Morocco, officials say
bullet_summary: The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said
Reported by CBS News, BBC World, France24 and others

items.title: 2 U.S. service members missing amid training exercise in Morocco
items.standfirst: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.
items.content: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.

items.title: Two US service members reported missing in Morocco, officials say
items.standfirst: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members, who are believed to have been involved in an accident, according to authorities.
items.content: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members, who are believed to have been involved in an accident, according to authorities.

items.title: Two US service members missing after joint military exercises in Morocco
items.standfirst: Two members of the US Armed Forces participating in the African Lion joint military exercises have been reported missing near the city of Tan Tan in southern Morocco, the US Africa Command (AFRICOM) said on Sunday. A US defence official said the soldiers went missing while on a hike after the day's exercises had concluded.
items.content: Two members of the US Armed Forces participating in the African Lion joint military exercises have been reported missing near the city of Tan Tan in southern Morocco, the US Africa Command (AFRICOM) said on Sunday. A US defence official said the soldiers went missing while on a hike after the day's exercises had concluded.

items.title: Two US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
items.standfirst: Search and rescue operation launched after service members reported missing near south-western city of Tan TanTwo US service members are missing in south-western Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (Africom) said on Sunday.The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said. Continue reading...
items.content: Search and rescue operation launched after service members reported missing near south-western city of Tan TanTwo US service members are missing in south-western Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (Africom) said on Sunday.The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said. Continue reading...

items.title: US military says two service members taking part in Morocco drills missing
items.standfirst: AFRICOM says search and rescue operations under way in southwestern Morocco during African Lion exercise.
items.content: AFRICOM says search and rescue operations under way in southwestern Morocco during African Lion exercise.

bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump said Saturday he was going to review a new Iranian peace proposal but said he doubted it would be acceptable, saying that Tehran had "not yet paid a big enough price"
Trump’s comments came after Iran's Tasnim and Fars news agencies said Tehran had submitted a 14-point proposal to mediator Pakistan

items.title: Bulk carrier reports 1st attack in weeks near Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Trump told reporters Saturday he is reviewing a new 14-point peace proposal that was submitted by Iran.
items.content: President Trump told reporters Saturday he is reviewing a new 14-point peace proposal that was submitted by Iran.

items.title: Trump casts doubt on Iran peace deal and says Tehran has not ‘paid a big enough price’
items.standfirst: US president to review Iranian proposal but signals scepticism amid deadlock and fears of renewed strikesDonald Trump has cast doubt on the prospect of a diplomatic breakthrough by claiming Iran had not yet “paid a big enough price” for its past wrongs.Trump’s remarks came amid growing speculation over the possibility of another round of US strikes against Iran aimed at forcing concessions, including a halt to the country’s nuclear programme. Continue reading...
items.content: US president to review Iranian proposal but signals scepticism amid deadlock and fears of renewed strikesDonald Trump has cast doubt on the prospect of a diplomatic breakthrough by claiming Iran had not yet “paid a big enough price” for its past wrongs.Trump’s remarks came amid growing speculation over the possibility of another round of US strikes against Iran aimed at forcing concessions, including a halt to the country’s nuclear programme. items.title: US President Trump says not likely to accept Iranian proposal
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Saturday he was going to review a new Iranian peace proposal but said he doubted it would be acceptable, saying that Tehran had "not yet paid a big enough price". Trump’s comments came after Iran's Tasnim and Fars news agencies said Tehran had submitted a 14-point proposal to mediator Pakistan. France 24 Correspondent Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Saturday he was going to review a new Iranian peace proposal but said he doubted it would be acceptable, saying that Tehran had "not yet paid a big enough price". Trump’s comments came after Iran's Tasnim and Fars news agencies said Tehran had submitted a 14-point proposal to mediator Pakistan. France 24 Correspondent Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

items.title: Trump says he is reviewing Iran's new offer but doubts it is 'acceptable'
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from Iran to end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed.“I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years,” the president wrote in a Truth Social post
items.content: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from Iran to end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed.“I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years,” the president wrote in a Truth Social post

items.standfirst: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".
items.content: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".

items.title: Investigators say Trump assassination suspect shot officer at press gala
items.standfirst: Secret Service agent at White House correspondents' dinner was 'definitively' hit by suspect's bullet, prosecutor says.
items.content: Secret Service agent at White House correspondents' dinner was 'definitively' hit by suspect's bullet, prosecutor says.

items.title: Jeanine Pirro says evidence shows suspect shot officer at White House press dinner
items.standfirst: US attorney says ‘we can establish’ pellet from defendant’s gunshot was ‘intertwined with fiber of vest’ of federal agentThe US government has evidence that a federal agent was shot by the suspect during an alleged recent attempt to assassinate Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner, the US attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro said on Sunday.“We now can establish that a pellet that came from the buckshot from the defendant’s Mossberg pump-action shotgun was intertwined with the fiber of the vest of the Secret Service officer,” Pirro said on CNN. Continue reading...
items.content: US attorney says ‘we can establish’ pellet from defendant’s gunshot was ‘intertwined with fiber of vest’ of federal agentThe US government has evidence that a federal agent was shot by the suspect during an alleged recent attempt to assassinate Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner, the US attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro said on Sunday.“We now can establish that a pellet that came from the buckshot from the defendant’s Mossberg pump-action shotgun was intertwined with the fiber of the vest of the Secret Service officer,” Pirro said on CNN. items.title: Two women die on migrant boat attempting to reach the UK from France
items.standfirst: Two young women died on Sunday while trying to cross from northern France to the UK in a small boat carrying some 82 people, a French government official said. French officials say that the number of people reaching Britain from France has dropped significantly this year compared with 2025.
items.content: Two young women died on Sunday while trying to cross from northern France to the UK in a small boat carrying some 82 people, a French government official said. clusters.title: Italian media reports US top diplomat Rubio to meet Pope Leo
clusters.neutral_headline: Italian media reports US top diplomat Rubio to meet Pope Leo
bullet_summary: Marco Rubio will travel to Rome this week for a visit reportedly aimed at easing strained relations with the Italyn government and the Holy See
Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: Italian media reports US top diplomat Rubio to meet Pope Leo
items.standfirst: Marco Rubio will travel to Rome this week for a visit reportedly aimed at easing strained relations with the Italyn government and the Holy See. Both Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo XIV have faced criticism from Donald Trump, who has publicly attacked the pontiff’s anti-war remarks. Marco Rubio is due to be in the Italian capital during the first anniversary of Pope Leo XIV’s papacy, the first US-born leader of the Catholic Church. France 24 correspondent Seema Gupta reports the latest from Rome.
items.content: Marco Rubio will travel to Rome this week for a visit reportedly aimed at easing strained relations with the Italyn government and the Holy See. Both Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo XIV have faced criticism from Donald Trump, who has publicly attacked the pontiff’s anti-war remarks. Marco Rubio is due to be in the Italian capital during the first anniversary of Pope Leo XIV’s papacy, the first US-born leader of the Catholic Church. France 24 correspondent Seema Gupta reports the latest from Rome.

items.title: Marco Rubio to visit Rome, reportedly to ‘thaw’ US relations with Pope and Meloni
items.standfirst: US secretary of state will be in Italian capital on Thursday and Friday, the one-year anniversary of Pope Leo’s papacyThe US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, is to travel to Rome this week for a visit reportedly aimed at thawing frosty relations with the Italian government and the Vatican.Rubio is scheduled to be in the Italian capital on Thursday and Friday, which will also mark the one-year anniversary of the papacy of Pope Leo, the first US-born pontiff. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate as brother fears she is dying
clusters.neutral_headline: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate as brother fears she is dying
bullet_summary: Her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health
Iran’s imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been urgently transferred from prison to hospital after what her foundation described as a severe deterioration in her health
She reportedly fainted twice on Friday

items.title: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate as brother fears she is dying
items.standfirst: Her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health.
items.content: Her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health.

items.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised
items.standfirst: Iran’s imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been urgently transferred from prison to hospital after what her foundation described as a severe deterioration in her health. She reportedly fainted twice on Friday. The incident follows a suspected heart attack in late March, raising growing concern among her supporters over her worsening condition.
items.content: Iran’s imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been urgently transferred from prison to hospital after what her foundation described as a severe deterioration in her health. She reportedly fainted twice on Friday. The incident follows a suspected heart attack in late March, raising growing concern among her supporters over her worsening condition.

clusters.title: Spain demands release of Gaza flotilla activists ‘held illegally’ by Israel
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain demands release of Gaza flotilla activists ‘held illegally’ by Israel
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera

items.title: Spain demands release of Gaza flotilla activists ‘held illegally’ by Israel
items.standfirst: Israeli court extends detention of two men who were among 175 people intercepted near Crete on ThursdaySpain’s foreign ministry has demanded the immediate release of a Spanish national it said was being “held illegally” by Israel after the interception of a Gaza-bound flotilla, hours after an Israeli court moved to extend his detention by two days.Saif Abu Keshek, who lives in Barcelona, and Thiago Ávila, from Brazil, appeared in court in Ashkelon on Sunday, days after Israeli forces intercepted at least 22 boats from a flotilla that was attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of the devastated Palestinian territory to deliver aid. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the 1st woman to train its winner
items.standfirst: Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.
items.content: Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.(Image credit: Jeff Roberson)

items.title: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal 'anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next
items.standfirst: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.
items.content: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.(Image credit: Markus Schreiber)

items.title: Trump administration to pull 5,000 troops from Germany
items.standfirst: The Pentagon will withdraw roughly 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, a move that comes amid President Trump’s growing clash with European allies over Iran and NATO tensions. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.
items.content: The Pentagon will withdraw roughly 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, a move that comes amid President Trump’s growing clash with European allies over Iran and NATO tensions. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.
